---
title: "Qualifier 3: Support Students in Their Choice and Navigation of Formal and Informal Learning Pathways"
---

::: qualifier-badge
Qualifier 3 · Learning Pathways
:::

I actively support students in navigating both formal and informal learning pathways by making learning processes explicit and providing structured opportunities for choice, progression, and self-directed development. A central principle of my curriculum design is that students should not only develop disciplinary knowledge, but should develop the ability to navigate their own learning — into the subject, through the degree, and into professional practice.

This is reflected in three interconnected dimensions: **transition into the curriculum**, **navigation through the course**, and **preparation for the workforce**.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Transition Into and Through the Curriculum

Within BIOL340, transition into the curriculum is supported through:

-   Clearly scaffolded practical sequences
-   Curated learning resources (step-by-step guides, annotated code, and exemplar outputs)
-   Flexible entry points that allow students to engage with material at an appropriate level of complexity

Recognising that many students arrive with limited experience in computational biology, the early weeks of BIOL340 are designed explicitly to support the transition from the more familiar laboratory science of BIOL215 into data-intensive analytical work. I normalise this transition in my teaching language and task design, framing the challenge of learning new tools as an expected and manageable part of becoming a biologist.

### Formal and Informal Pathways

Students are encouraged to move between **formal learning environments** (scheduled labs, workshops, and assessments) and **informal pathways**, such as independent practice, peer collaboration, and extension materials embedded within the subject's Moodle site.

A key strategy is the integration of staged assessment and reflective tasks, which support students in understanding not only *what* they are learning, but *how* they are learning. The transition from a structured scientific report to a **generative AI reflection task** requires students to evaluate their own learning processes, tool use, and decision-making — promoting **metacognitive awareness** and helping students navigate increasingly independent learning contexts.

> *"They \[the BIOL340 wet and dry practicals\] worked very well together and had a good flow between them. They built knowledge from a more simple to a more complex level, and aligned well with the content presented in similar weeks." —* BIOL340 student 2025.

------------------------------------------------------------------------

## Preparation for the Workforce

A deliberate focus of my curriculum redesign in both BIOL340 and CHEM325 has been the explicit framing of **computational and digital literacy as essential graduate skills** for modern biologists. As biology becomes increasingly data-driven, graduates are expected to engage with real datasets, use analytical tools confidently, and communicate findings in ways that reflect contemporary scientific practice.

My curriculum redesign is premised on this reality: that preparing students for the workforce requires embedding the tools, workflows, and ways of thinking they will encounter in research and industry roles.

This framing is reflected in student feedback. In response to the personalised survey question *"This subject has made me feel equipped to work in the field of Cell and Molecular Biology,"* students in BIOL340 returned:

-   Mean score: **4.75/6.0**
-   **80%** agreeing or strongly agreeing

Qualitative responses further reinforce this connection — students noted in response to "*Were these computer activities valuable and would you like to see more of this kind of learning in the future"* that computational activities were valuable because *"it is what we will be using in the industry"* and that the subject reflected *"current techniques."*

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Evidence:** BIOL340 pre- and post-dry-practical surveys and student evaluation reports are available in the evidence folder. See also the [BIOL340 teaching website](https://nikolasjohnston.github.io/BIOL340_DP/pages/DP1).

------------------------------------------------------------------------

**Previous:** [Qualifier 2 — Inclusive Learning](cpd2.html) · **Next:** [Qualifier 4 — Scaffolded Assessment](cpd4.html)
